business.headline: India posts Q4 current account surplus as services exports surgeAccording to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Monday, the country's current account recorded a surplus of USD 7.1 billion, or 0.7 per cent of GDP, in the fourth quarter of 2025-26.

Oil, growth, inflation: CEA explains India's key risks amid West Asia crisisSpeaking to India Today, CEA V Anantha Nageswaran said India's economy remains strong, but uncertainty around the ongoing conflict and energy prices continues to pose risks.

Air India may introduce 'no meals' tickets on domestic flights to cut faresAir India is considering a no-meals ticket category for domestic passengers. The plan could begin on select short routes and lower fares for flyers who skip onboard food.

Builder delayed possession? Here's what homebuyers can doBuilder delayed possession of your flat? Learn your rights under RERA, when you can seek compensation or a refund, whether you must continue paying EMI, and what legal and financial options homebuyers have when projects are delayed.

Oil prices cannot stay this high for long, says Oil Minister Hardeep Singh PuriWhile fuel prices remain elevated for now, Puri's comments suggest that policymakers expect the pressure to ease over time.

Want to invest in SpaceX's IPO? Here's what retail investors need to knowAccess to the SpaceX IPO is not limited to the United States. The company has indicated that qualified investors in several countries may be able to buy shares, subject to local regulations and eligibility requirements.

Sensex, Nifty hit 2-month lows as Iran-Israel tensions push crude oil near $100Markets closed at nearly two-month lows after fresh Israeli strikes on Iran and Lebanon pushed crude prices sharply higher. The sell-off deepened across sectors as investors weighed inflation risks, global tech weakness and the path of interest rates.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

From AI trip planning to travel insurance: How are Indians redefining travel in 2026?Gone are the days when a holiday meant simply booking tickets and packing bags. Today's Indian traveller is more digital, more informed and more prepared, reshaping the travel landscape in the process.

India, US could finalise trade deal by July, says Commerce Minister Piyush GoyalThe Union Minister's remarks come at a time when India and the US are working to resolve outstanding issues in a proposed bilateral trade pact that is expected to improve market access and give Indian exporters an advantage over competitors in certain sectors.

AI stock sell-off hits Taiwan, South Korea after rally past IndiaAsian markets sank as investors dumped AI-linked chip stocks in Taiwan, South Korea and Japan. The sell-off has raised fresh questions over whether AI rally expectations had run ahead of earnings reality.

CIBIL score under 730? Getting a home or car loan could become tougher from 2027For many Indians, a home loan or car loan is often the biggest financial commitment they make. But from 2027, getting such loans could become more difficult if your CIBIL score is below 730, as banks prepare to follow stricter RBI guidelines on lending risk.

Adani Ports bags 10-year marine services deal for Argentina's first LNG exportsThe contract was awarded to Adani Harbour International FZCO, a step-down subsidiary of APSEZ, through a consortium formed with Argentina-based Meridian Group. The consortium won the contract after a global competitive bidding process conducted by Southern Energy S.A. (SESA).

Gold falls by Rs 1,800, silver crashes Rs 6,400: Why are precious metals falling?Gold and silver extended losses on Monday as stronger US jobs data altered rate expectations. The slide deepened as higher Treasury yields, Middle East tensions and inflation worries weighed on bullion.

Sensex falls 600 points: 3 reasons why stock market is falling todayMarkets opened sharply lower on Monday as rising Middle East tensions, higher crude prices and global weakness hit sentiment. The slide sharpened concerns over inflation, foreign outflows and pressure on technology stocks.

Stock market falls amid fresh Iran-Israel flare up, crude oil climbs above $96The selloff was broad-based, with almost all major stocks trading in the red. Investors turned cautious after fresh conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and raised concerns about inflation and global economic growth.
Stock markets likely to fall today: 3 reasons whyGIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,138 at 8:06 am IST, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open about 1% lower than its previous close of 23,366.70.

Crude oil rises over 3%. Is $100 a barrel back in sight?Brent crude rose 3.45% to $96.30 per barrel, while US benchmark WTI crude climbed 3.41% to $93.63 per barrel during trading as of 8:10 am. The sharp rebound erased most of Friday's losses, when oil prices had fallen on hopes that diplomatic efforts could ease tensions in the region.

Your flat is delayed. Your EMI isn't. What now?You're paying the EMI. The builder missed the deadline. With over 5 lakh homes stuck across India, delayed possession has become a financial nightmare for thousands of buyers. Here's what experts say you can do when your dream home doesn't arrive on time.

Rosneft CEO predicts India will drive 50% of global oil demand growth by 2035Rosneft CEO cited International Energy Agency estimates projecting that India's oil consumption would reach nearly eight million barrels per day by 2035, representing growth of 44 per cent, while global demand overall would increase by about five per cent.

entertainment.headline: Varun Dhawan's proposal to Natasha did not go as planned. Actor spills details Varun Dhawan recalled how his proposal to Natasha Dalal in Alibaug did not go as planned and called it a bad proposal. The actor also said real-life proposals are often very different from what films show.

Nora Fatehi's FIFA World Cup track Siir Siir out: 'A full-circle moment' Nora Fatehi's latest track, Siir Siir, has been released as part of the official FIFA World Cup 2026 music project. The actor-performer also opened up about the song's cultural roots and the strong Indian representation behind it.

Rs 3,300 for a movie ticket? The Odyssey mania is already sweeping India Advance bookings for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey opened across India with IMAX ticket prices touching Rs 3,300 in some cities. Despite the steep rates, several shows sold out within minutes, sparking debate and excitement online.

Padma Awards 2026: Mammootty, R Madhavan among film stars to be honoured on June 23 Mammootty, R Madhavan, Satish Shah and Rajendra Prasad are among the film personalities who will receive Padma honours from President Droupadi Murmu on June 23.

SlumDog 33 Temple Road teaser: Vijay Sethupathi turns a mysterious blind beggar The teaser of Puri Jagannadh's SlumDog: 33 Temple Road introduces Vijay Sethupathi as a visually impaired beggar. Billed as a pan-India release, the official release date of the upcoming film is yet to be announced by the makers.

Farah Khan recalls compliment from Michael Jackson: Still getting goosebumps Farah Khan recalled meeting Michael Jackson and the compliment he gave her during a recent vlog. She said the brief encounter still gives her goosebumps and remains one of her most cherished memories.

Idris Elba says James Bond shouldn't be "woke": Let's not try it Idris Elba said that the reports of him playing James Bond were never legitimate. He said the franchise should stay focused on escapism rather than chasing changing tastes.

Mom-to-be Deepika Padukone checks in on dream Bandra home with Ranveer Singh. Pics Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh checked the progress of their new Bandra home on Monday. Balcony pictures from the visit grabbed attention online for the actor's pregnancy glow and baby bump.

Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma welcomes rock band Guns N' Roses to Guwahati Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma welcomed the iconic rock Guns N' Roses to Guwahati for its upcoming concert. He said the show builds on recent momentum for Assam as a live entertainment destination and could boost tourism and the local economy.

Targeting Janhvi in Peddi row is unfair, she did what director said: Jagapathi Babu Actor Jagapathi Babu has defended Janhvi Kapoor amid online criticism over her role in Peddi, urging fans not to target her personally. He also said that an artist merely follows the director's orders, and it is unfair for her to be subjected to online trolling.

Karisma Kapoor on 90s Bollywood: No scripts, hardly any rehearsals Karisma Kapoor looked back on 1990s Hindi cinema and said many films were made without detailed scripts. She said the era stood out for its spontaneity, camaraderie and enduring superhits.

Anil Kapoor to host Indian adaptation of global game show The 1% Club Actor Anil Kapoor is set to return to television as the host of Star Plus' Indian adaptation of the global hit game show The 1% Club. The actor is currently shooting for the first promo, with an official announcement expected soon.

Amid Peddi row, Kangana Ranaut says the lens of movies has long been harsh on women Amid the Peddi row, Kangana Ranaut said cinema has often portrayed women harshly and acknowledged concerns about their representation. However, she cautioned against judging every depiction in isolation or fuelling online outrage.

Dharna Durga's journey from mimicry videos to films amid personal loss Maa Behen actor Dharna Durga's journey from Instagram Reels to Bollywood has been shaped by personal loss, family responsibility and years of hard work. Here's how the influencer-turned-actor made her way from lockdown videos to the big screen.

Shoaib Ibrahim dismisses Dipika's surgery rumours: Her immunotherapy has started Shoaib Ibrahim said Dipika Kakar has begun immunotherapy and has not been advised surgery for now. He also shared that his father is recovering gradually after brain surgery.

How Bollywood ruined 'ladka ladki dost ho sakte hain' for an entire generation As Cocktail 2 heads to cinemas, it once again turns friendship into romance. The familiar trope has revived questions over Bollywood's reluctance to depict healthy platonic bonds.

Actor Jisshu Sengupta reveals the Rituparno Ghosh advice that changed his career Jisshu Sengupta recalled how a question from Rituparno Ghosh changed his approach to choosing roles. He said the lesson pushed him towards characters essential to the story rather than a conventional hero image.

The many lives of Salim Kumar: Comedian, actor, director and cultural icon With the death of Salim Kumar, Malayalam cinema bids farewell to an artiste whose journey from mimicry stages to National Award-winning performances left an indelible mark on Mollywood.

Champion director Pradeep Advaitham backs Peddi amid row, calls reviewers idiots Backing Peddi amid the ongoing controversy, Champion director Pradeep Advaitham criticised reviewers for misreading the film's themes and social context. Though he agreed that the heroine's track could have been better, he praised the film's storytelling and Ram Charan's performance.

general news.headline: Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee for skilled foreign workers thrown out by US judgeUS District Judge Leo Sorokin in Boston on Monday struck down the H-1B visa fee, which Trump announced in September as part of immigration crackdown.

Have some shame and spine: Mahua Moitra slams Yusuf Pathan as TMC MPs rebelMahua Moitra's remarks came hours after 20 Lok Sabha MPs, led by Chief Whip Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, wrote to Speaker Om Birla declaring support for the BJP-led NDA, triggering a split in the TMC's parliamentary unit.

Alcohol sales allowed in Muslim-majority Lakshadweep. Explained why rules changedFor nearly five decades, Lakshadweep was one of India's few territories where alcohol sales were largely prohibited. Around 97% of the population is Muslim. The Centre is now changing that, bringing a regulated licencing system as part of a broader push to boost tourism.

At INDIA meet, allies confront Congress, Rahul Gandhi explains: Inside detailsEarlier in the day, leaders of 25 Opposition parties met in New Delhi to discuss their strategy to take on the BJP-led government over several issues, including SIR, the NEET-CBSE exam row, and the ongoing economic situation.

Israel plans a Shivaji statue. Untold story of Jews who fought in Maratha armyIsrael is preparing to erect a statue of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in the Jewish country, and bringing to spotlight an overlooked historical connection between Jews and India. The story stretches from the villages of Maharashtra to the armies and navies that helped shape India's history.

Apple announces Siri AI, it is powered by next-gen Apple IntelligenceAt WWDC 2026, Apple unveiled Siri AI, bringing its biggest Siri upgrade yet with the power of AI alongside the next generation of Apple Intelligence and iOS 27.

Several Indian workers killed in Dubai as minibus rams broken-down truckDubai Police said preliminary findings suggest the accident occurred after a truck developed a technical malfunction and came to a halt on the roadway. The minibus travelling behind it then collided with the vehicle.

Shinde Vs Fadnavis? How a missing invite exposed deep rift within MahayutiEknath Shinde was not invited to the Shivaji statue unveiling at Navi Mumbai International Airport. The omission prompted protests from his camp, opposition jibes and CMO notices over protocol.

They said you'd never win: Djokovic writes moving tribute to champion ZverevFollowing Alexander Zverev's historic French Open breakthrough, Novak Djokovic wrote a deeply personal tribute to his long-time friend, lauding the German's resilience in silencing critics who claimed he would never win a Grand Slam.

England cricket probe Ben Stokes, Gus Atkinson after Lord's nightclub incidentEngland captain Ben Stokes and pacer Gus Atkinson are under investigation after an alleged breach of team protocols following the first Test against New Zealand. The incident has delayed squad decisions for the second Test and revived scrutiny of England's off-field culture.

Ex-Bombay High Court judge receives threatening letters over Dawoodi Bohra verdictIn April 2024, Justice Gautam Patel had upheld the position of Syedna Mufaddal Saifuddin as the 53rd Dai al-Mutlaq (leader) of the Dawoodi Bohra community, while noting that he had a valid appointment.

Use public transport: Bandra Kurla Complex's Friday fix for its traffic headacheMMRDA and WRI India have launched a weekly public transport drive for BKC commuters every Friday. The move seeks to ease congestion as more commuters signal a willingness to shift from private vehicles.

Babur's sultanate: Parasite on a prosperous civilisation?India was already one of the world's richest civilisations when Babur arrived in 1526. Five hundred years later, the question isn't about imperial splendour during the Timurid rule of Hindustan, but whether they extracted more wealth than they created.

POK on edge as 11 killed, 70 injured in violent clashes ahead of major protestAt least 11 people were killed and more than 70 injured during clashes between protesters and security forces in Rawalakot ahead of a region-wide shutdown planned for June 9, according to news agency Reuters.

1 lakh customers bought this SUV: Offers 28kmpl efficiency, price starts at Rs 10.50 lakhMaruti Suzuki's latest SUV has crossed the 1 lakh sales milestone in just eight months. Priced from Rs 10.50 lakh and offering up to 28.65kmpl efficiency, the SUV has quickly become a strong seller.

Africa's top referee denied US entry before FIFA World CupAfrica's top referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan has reportedly been denied entry into the United States and sent back to Turkey ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident has intensified concerns over visa and travel issues affecting tournament personnel.

Sonam Wangchuk from Ladakh. The lion, not the cockroachMany know Sonam Wangchuk, the climate activist and educationist, who backed the Cockroach Janta Party protest at Jantar Mantar. But not many know the other Sonam Wangchuk, who's also from Ladakh. Colonel Sonam Wangchuk, the 'Lion of Ladakh', fought in the icy heights during the 1999 Kargil War.

People will suffocate and die: Court on govt's plan to take over Delhi Polo ClubThis case comes close on the heels of the Centre asking the century-old Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its 27.3-acre premises in the heart of Lutyens' Delhi by June 5, citing its requirement for defence infrastructure and other official purposes.

8 workers killed after ladle carrying molten steel explodes in Vizag steel plantEight workers were killed, and several others injured after a ladle carrying molten steel exploded at the Visakhapatnam Steel Plant's SMS-2 and STC-3 facility in Andhra Pradesh, triggering a major fire and panic among workers.
Indian astronaut Papa Nair awarded Kirti Chakra for extraordinary courageOne of the faces of India's ambitious Gaganyaan mission has received one of the country's highest peacetime gallantry honours.

Rs 3,300 for a movie ticket? The Odyssey mania is already sweeping IndiaAdvance bookings for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey opened across India with IMAX ticket prices touching Rs 3,300 in some cities. Despite the steep rates, several shows sold out within minutes, sparking debate and excitement online.

Monsoon to advance into Karnataka; north India braces for heat on TuesdayThe southwest monsoon entered parts of Karnataka, Maharashtra and Andhra Pradesh on June 8. The shift is set to bring heavier rain to the west and south while north India stays largely hot and dry.

Influencers claim nicotine is not the villain. So, did we get it wrong all this time?Social media influencers are pitching nicotine as a wellness aid when it comes to improving brain function. But how far or close are we to the truth? Is nicotine really not as dangerous? We asked experts.

Ship with 24 Indian seafarers hit in alleged US Navy strike near Oman; crew safeA merchant vessel, MT MARIVEX, carrying 24 Indian seafarers is sinking near Oman's Masirah Island after a fire incident reportedly linked to an alleged US Navy strike.

sports.headline: You're a gift to the sport: Andre Agassi hails Chwalinska's Roland Garros run Andre Agassi delivered an emotional message to Maja Chwalinska after her dream run to the Roland Garros final came to an end. The American legend hailed the Polish qualifier's historic achievement and called her "a gift to the sport."

England cricket probe Ben Stokes, Gus Atkinson after Lord's nightclub incident England captain Ben Stokes and pacer Gus Atkinson are under investigation after an alleged breach of team protocols following the first Test against New Zealand. The incident has delayed squad decisions for the second Test and revived scrutiny of England's off-field culture.

Mandaviya backs club-led model to secure ISL future Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya has asked the AIFF and ISL clubs to form a joint task force to settle their financial dispute. The move is aimed at securing a stable business model before the 2026-27 season begins.

Africa's top referee denied US entry before FIFA World Cup Africa's top referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan has reportedly been denied entry into the United States and sent back to Turkey ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident has intensified concerns over visa and travel issues affecting tournament personnel.

They said you'd never win: Djokovic writes moving tribute to champion Zverev Following Alexander Zverev's historic French Open breakthrough, Novak Djokovic wrote a deeply personal tribute to his long-time friend, lauding the German's resilience in silencing critics who claimed he would never win a Grand Slam.

Shreyaka takes 4 as India spin to win against West Indies in T20 World Cup warm-up Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Bharti Fulmali's unbeaten fifty and Shreyanka Patil's four wickets gave India a 26-run warm-up win over West Indies in Cardiff. The outing offered useful game time to India's bench before matches against England and Pakistan.

Suryakumar Yadav answers T20I snub with Mumbai T20 League blitz Dropped from India's T20I squads for Ireland and England, Suryakumar Yadav struck an unbeaten 72 in the Mumbai T20 League at Wankhede. The innings revived his run of form and renewed focus on his place in India's plans.

India's delicate plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Help his game, not ruin it India's batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will be eased into the national set-up after his maiden T20I call-up. The team wants to protect the 15-year-old's natural game rather than overload him with advice.

Test team in transition? Sunil Gavaskar disagrees with coach Gautam Gambhir's take As India enter a crucial World Test Championship phase, batting legend Sunil Gavaskar has urged the young, evolving batting unit to step up and focus on long-form discipline rather than relying on the transition narrative.

Christian Eriksen in good spirits, set for discharge after mid-match heart scare Christian Eriksen is expected to be discharged from hospital soon after making a positive recovery from a mid-match collapse during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine. The Danish midfielder quickly regained consciousness, with doctors confirming he remains in stable condition.

Yogasana Super League: An IPL-themed competition to boost yoga's Olympic dream Yogasana Bharat says the Yogasana Super League is nearly ready for launch this year. The league sits at the centre of a broader push for Asian Games, Commonwealth Games and Olympic recognition.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rise is dangerous for cricket: Greg ChappellFormer Australia cricketer Greg Chappell has warned that T20 cricket is approaching a tipping point, arguing that the format has become heavily skewed in favour of batters. Concerned about the growing imbalance between bat and ball, Chappell proposed three rule changes that he believes could restore balance to the game.

Gill reveals India's 350-run, 20-wicket Test formula after AFG routShubman Gill laid out India's 350-run, 20-wicket Test formula after the innings win over Afghanistan in Mullanpur. The result underlined India's bench strength and highlighted Manav Suthar's dream debut in a side in transition.

Manav Suthar stays grounded despite seven-wicket India Test debutManav Suthar starred on his India Test debut as his seven wickets sealed a crushing win over Afghanistan. Yet the left-arm spinner said the format demands patience, consistency and humility.

Innings and 300 runs: Suthar blows away Afghans inside 3 days on Test debutShubman Gill's India registered their biggest victory in Test cricket on Monday, June 8. Debutant Manav Suthar blew away Afghanistan with 7 wickets in Mullanpur.

Ignored for India, Shami makes his presence felt with hattrick in Bengal T20 LeagueMohammed Shami took a Bengal T20 League hat-trick for Siliguri Strikers against Rarh Tigers. The spell revived scrutiny of India's selection calls as the veteran remains outside ODI and Test plans.

Angry Mohammed Siraj riles up Rahmat Shah, triggers Afghanistan collapseIND vs AFG: Mohammed Siraj triggered Afghanistan's collapse on Day 3 of the Test match. He did not do it with his bat or ball, but with his mere words.

India captain snaps back at reporter over retirement question at Women's T20 WCHarmanpreet Kaur brushed aside retirement talk with a joke at a pre-tournament captains' media session. The exchange kept the focus on India's T20 World Cup build-up and the wider lift success has given women's cricket at home.

CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa, impresses Norway Chess championTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay felicitated R Praggnanandhaa in Chennai, awarded him Rs 50 lakh and played an impromptu game of chess. The meeting followed the grandmaster's historic Norway Chess title win, with Praggnanandhaa saying he was surprised by the Chief Minister's skill.

Manav Suthar becomes 2nd Indian this century to pick 5 wickets on Test debutIndia's debutant left-arm spinner ripped through Afghanistan with 6/33 and helped enforce the follow-on. The spell, backed by a handy 28, put him among India's best Test debutants.

Let Sooryavanshi be, don't expect the world of him: Sourav GangulyFormer India captain Sourav Ganguly has asked the world to temper his expectations ahead of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi potential India debut. Ganguly has said that playing for India will be different from playing in the IPL.

Flavio Cobolli follows Andreeva's example, thanks himself after French Open finalFrench Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli thanked himself and praised Alexander Zverev after a five-set French Open final defeat. The loss ended his title bid but confirmed a breakthrough fortnight that changed his standing in the game.

In Roland Garros, Zverev finds freedom in maiden Grand Slam winGermany's Alexander Zverev finally won his maiden Grand Slam. Playing against Italy's Flavio Cobolli, Zverev finally found the freedom that he had been looking for.

Why Praggnanandhaa's win in Norway Chess 2026 brings back belief of good times in Indian Chess?Norway Chess 2026: Praggnanandhaa created history when he won the Norway Chess 2026 event in grand style. Not only did he become the first Indian to win the prestigious tournament, he also defeated Magnus Carlsen in his sentinel twice and that deserves a special mention in the annals of chess.

Only Test, Day 3 Live: India win by innings and 100 runs in New ChandigarhIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) Only Test Live Score and Updates, Day 3: It's all over in New Chandigarh. Afghanistan have lost by an innings and 300 runs. The Test match ends inside three days. Washington Sundar, Manav Suthar and Kuldeep Yadav run through the visitors' batting line-up.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the summer of suspended disbeliefHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the summer of 2026, a boy from Bihar walked to the crease and gave Indian cricket back something it had quietly lost: the pure, uncomplicated joy of watching without needing to explain why.

Alexander Zverev leaves painful past behind, makes French Open his happy placeFrench Open 2026: Alexander Zverev has endured a difficult history at Roland Garros, including a serious injury in 2022 and a defeat in the 2024 final. But on Sunday, the German finally turned the page in Paris, capturing his first Grand Slam title and ending a long wait.

From losers to Grand Slam champions: Zverev pays emotional tribute to his teamAlexander Zverev finally ended his long pursuit of Grand Slam glory on Sunday, defeating Flavio Cobolli to win the French Open before thanking the team that stood beside him through injuries, heartbreak and repeated near-misses on tennis's biggest stages.

Neymar's last dance: Final shot at redemption or a farewell to Joga Bonito?FIFA World Cup 2026: With injuries recurring, Brazil's decision to bank on Neymar for what could be his final World Cup has reopened the old argument about promise, decline and necessity. Can Carlo Ancelotti's call turn a fading icon into the centre of Brazil's latest reckoning with hope and history?

Christian Eriksen collapses on pitch again, five years after cardiac arrestChristian Eriksen sparked fresh concern on Sunday after the Denmark midfielder collapsed on the pitch during an international friendly against Ukraine, forcing the match to be abandoned.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
